prompt: My KOG and KM shares on ASK are worth about 1.5M NOK total and make up roughly 85% of my investment portfolio. I know that's concentrated but I work at KM and believe in the company. Should I diversify? If so, how do I do it tax-efficiently? And what does this concentration risk actually mean for reaching financial freedom?
assertions: Flags 85% concentration as exceeding the 30% single-position threshold
Explains ASK tax rules: tax-free switching inside ASK, only taxed on withdrawal above total deposits
Notes the additional risk of having both employment income AND portfolio concentrated in the same company
Presents bull case (KOG/KM defense/maritime growth) and bear case (sector downturn, layoffs + portfolio loss simultaneously)
Considers gradual diversification vs immediate rebalancing
Models FI timeline impact of different portfolio allocations (concentrated vs diversified)
Conservative bias visible — recommends reducing concentration even if bull case is strong
